nodes.description: Information about the reuse system.

nodes.label: componentName
nodes.description:
Name of the component. If the product has only one component (the product itself), a comment is enough.

This field must be used where the product consists multiple components, and where these components are made of different materials as assigned by the manufacturer of a product.

nodes.label: componentCode
nodes.description: An identifier assigned to the component.

nodes.label: componentDescription
nodes.description: Short description of component, including function or specification. Information can include data about component type, weight.

nodes.label: componentLocations
nodes.description: Locations or positions of the component within the product.

nodes.label: componentSortingInformation
nodes.description: Description of how to sort the component.

nodes.label: componentPassportIdentifier
nodes.description: The identifier of the component passport, which is a uuidv4.

nodes.label: recycled
nodes.description: The share of the material, that is recovered recycled content from the product. his attribute is mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products Annex I: (h) use or content of recycled materials and recovery of materials, including critical raw materials, Proposal for a REGULATION OF THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT AND OF THE COUNCIL on circularity requirements for vehicle design and on management of end-of-life vehicles, amending Regulations (EU) 2018/858 and 2019/1020 and repealing Directives 2000/53/EC and 2005/64/EC: Article 10: Declaration on recycled content present in vehicles. Manufacturers shall declare, for each vehicle type that is type-approved as of OP: Please insert the date = the first day of the month following 36 months after the entry into force of the Regulation under Regulation (EU) 2018/858, the respective share of recycled content of:(a)neodymium, dysprosium, praseodymium, terbium, samarium, boron in permanent magnets in e-drive motors, (b)aluminium and its alloys, (c)magnesium and its alloys, (d)steel. The declaration shall concern the recycled content of these materials present in the vehicle type and indicate, per material share, whether the material is recycled from pre-consumer waste or from post-consumer waste.

nodes.label: materialIdentifications
nodes.description: The chemical material name and identification, in accordance with the specification in the attribute for the list type. Preference is given to the IUPAC name. This attribute is mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products Article 7: 5. Unless otherwise provided for under paragraph 6, point (b), the information requirements shall make it possible to track the substances of concern, throughout the life cycle of the products concerned, unless such tracking is already possible pursuant to information requirements laid down in another delegated act adopted pursuant to Article 4 covering the products concerned, and shall include at least the following: (a) the name or numerical code of the substances of concern present in the product, as follows: (i) name in the International Union of Pure and Applied Chemistry (IUPAC) nomenclature, or another international name when IUPAC name is not available (ii) other names, including usual name, trade name, abbreviation,(iii) European Community (EC) number, as indicated in the European Inventory of Existing Commercial Chemical Substances (EINECS), the European List of Notified Chemical Substances (ELINCS) or the No Longer Polymer (NLP) list or the number assigned by the European Chemicals Agency (ECHA), if available and appropriate, (iv) the Chemical Abstract Service (CAS) name and number, if available.

nodes.label: materialDocuments
nodes.description: Documentation accompanying the material.

nodes.label: substancesOfConcernInformation
nodes.description:
Information regarding substances of concern in the product. The ESPR provisional agreement from January 9th, 2024 defines:
(52) 'hazardous substance' means a substance classified as hazardous pursuant to Article 3 of Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008.

nodes.label: materialType
nodes.description: Information refers to the specific category of material intentionally or unintentionally incorporated into the product. Can inform about multi-constituent substances or other aspects that can be associated with the proper identification of material or informing about Substance of Unknown or Variable composition, Complex reaction products or Biological material.

nodes.label: materialOrigin
nodes.description:
Country of material origin. 2 letter code from ISO 3166.

Attribute is mentioned in Regulation (EU) 2017/821 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 17 May 2017 laying down supply chain due diligence obligations for Union importers of tin, tantalum and tungsten, their ores, and gold originating from conflict-affected and high-risk areas. 
Article 4: Management system obligations
Union importers of minerals or metals shall:
(a) adopt, and clearly communicate to suppliers and the public up-to-date information on, their supply chain policy for the minerals and metals potentially originating from conflict-affected and high-risk areas 

This attribute is mentioned in: COMMUNICATION FROM THE COMMISSION TO THE EUROPEAN PARLIAMENT, THE COUNCIL, THE EUROPEAN ECONOMIC AND SOCIAL COMMITTEE AND THE COMMITTEE OF THE REGIONS EU Strategy for Sustainable and Circular Textiles 
2.4.Introducing information requirements and a Digital Product Passport
 To ensure consistency with this new piece of legislation, the Commission will also review the Textile Labelling Regulation, which requires textiles sold on the EU market to carry a label clearly identifying the fibre composition and indicating any non-textile parts of animal origin. As part of this review and subject to an impact assessment, the Commission will introduce mandatory disclosure of other types of information, such as sustainability and circularity parameters, products  size and, where applicable, the country where manufacturing processes take place ( made in ).

Directive (EU) 2024/1760 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 on corporate sustainability due diligence and amending Directive (EU) 2019/1937 and Regulation (EU) 2023/2859 also demand in non
financial statement this kind of information.

This attribute often appear in different voluntary certification and labelling schemes.

nodes.label: materialPassportIdentifier
nodes.description: The identifier of the material passport, which is a uuidv4.

nodes.label: materialLocations
nodes.description: Locations/positions of the material within the product.

nodes.label: declarableIngredient
nodes.description:
Declaration of ingredients contained within a product, product part, or material as applicable. The information required to determine product compliance with ingredients regulations, standards, and market needs. 
Examples of typical ingredient lists that could be reported depend on the regulatory standard requirements or restricted substances lists (e.g., AFIRM, GADSL), limitation of concentrations :
Critical raw materials, Renewable Materials, Compostable Materials, Microorganisms, Alergens, Fragrances, Ingredients in surfactants ordered by concentration ranges, Textile fibers, Annex IV of the POP Regulation, declaration of articles with SVHC above 0,1%, etc., declaration regarding microplastics or nanomaterials.

Examples are renewable materials:
The share of the material, which is from a renewable resource that can be replenished. These are the kind of resources that can be regenerated throughout time. Forest wood, for example, can be grown through reforestation. This attribute is mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, Annex I:
(i) use or content of sustainable renewable materials 

Examples are critical raw materials: 
This attribute is mentioned in Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, Annex I:
(h) use or content of recycled materials and recovery of materials, including critical raw materials 
In Annex II of the connected Regulation (EU) 2024/1252 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 11 April 2024 establishing a framework for ensuring a secure and sustainable supply of critical raw materials, a list of critical raw materials can be found.

nodes.label: concentrationInformation
nodes.description: Information about the concentration of the material at the product level.

nodes.label: collectionPointIdentification
nodes.description: The identifier used for a location.

nodes.label: collectionPointAddress
nodes.description: Full address of the collection point for reusable products.

nodes.label: reuseSystemInformation
nodes.description: Documentation and identification of the reuse system.

nodes.label: materialName
nodes.description: The name of the material that is present in the product.

nodes.label: materialId
nodes.description: The material identification, in accordance with the specification in the attribute for the list type.

nodes.label: materialListTypeId
nodes.description: The type of standard used for the identification of the substances. Selected can be for example CAS, IUPAC or EC.

nodes.label: substanceOfConcern
nodes.description: Information regarding substance of concern in the product. Attributes are among others substance names, ids, concentration, location and hazard class. The Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products defines: (27) substance of concern means a substance that: (a) meets the criteria laid down in Article 57 of Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 and is identified in accordance with Article 59(1) of that Regulation; (b) is classified in Part 3 of Annex VI to Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 in one of the following hazard classes or hazard categories:(i) carcinogenicity categories 1 and 2;(ii) germ cell mutagenicity categories 1 and 2; (iii) reproductive toxicity categories 1 and 2; (iv) endocrine disruption for human health categories 1 and 2; (v) endocrine disruption for the environment categories 1 and 2; (vi) persistent, mobile and toxic or very persistent, very mobile properties; (vii) persistent, bioaccumulative and toxic or very persistent, very bioaccumulative properties; (viii) respiratory sensitisation category 1; (ix) skin sensitisation category 1; (x) hazardous to the aquatic environment- categories chronic 1 to 4; (xi) hazardous to the ozone layer; (xii) specific target organ toxicity -repeated exposure categories 1 and 2; (xiii) specific target organ toxicity -single exposure categories 1 and 2; (c) is regulated under Regulation (EU) 2019/1021; or (d) negatively affects the reuse and recycling of materials in the product in which it is present;.

nodes.label: declarableIngredientLists
nodes.description:
Declaration of ingredients contained within a product, product part, or material as applicable. The information required to determine product compliance with ingredients regulations, standards, and market needs. 
Examples of typical ingredient lists that could be reported depend on the regulatory standard requirements or restricted substances lists (e.g., AFIRM, GADSL), limitation of concentrations :
Critical raw materials, Renewable Materials, Compostable Materials, Microorganisms, Alergens, Fragrances, Ingredients in surfactants ordered by concentration ranges, Textile fibers, Annex IV of the POP Regulation, declaration of articles with SVHC above 0,1%, etc., declaration regarding microplastics or nanomaterials.

Examples are renewable materials:
The share of the material, which is from a renewable resource that can be replenished. These are the kind of resources that can be regenerated throughout time. Forest wood, for example, can be grown through reforestation. This attribute is mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, Annex I:
(i) use or content of sustainable renewable materials;

Examples are critical raw materials: 
This attribute is mentioned in Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, Annex I:
(h) use or content of recycled materials and recovery of materials, including critical raw materials;
In Annex II of the connected Regulation (EU) 2024/1252 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 11 April 2024 establishing a framework for ensuring a secure and sustainable supply of critical raw materials, a list of critical raw materials can be found.

nodes.label: concentration
nodes.description: Concentration of the material at the level of the product.

nodes.label: concentrationRange
nodes.description: The concentration range for the substance of concern. This attribute is specially mentioned for substances of concern mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, Article 7: (5)(c) the concentration, maximum concentration or concentration range of the substances of concern, at the level of the product, its relevant components, or spare parts.

nodes.label: reuseSystemIdentification
nodes.description: Identification of the reuse system.

nodes.label: symbolsOfDepositAndReturnSystem
nodes.preferredName: Symbols Of Deposit And Return System
nodes.description: Document of the symbols of the deposit and return system.

nodes.label: rotation
nodes.description:
Rotation  means the cycle that reusable packaging accomplishes from the moment it is placed on the market together with the product it is intended to contain, protect, handle, deliver or present to the moment it is ready to be re-used within a re-use system with a view to it being supplied again to end users together with another product.

This definition is based on the following regulation:
Regulation (EU) 2025/40 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 19 December 2024 on packaging and packaging waste, amending Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 and Directive (EU) 2019/904, and repealing Directive 94/62/EC

nodes.label: trip
nodes.description:
Trip  means the transfer of packaging, from filling or loading to emptying or unloading, as part of a rotation or on its own.

This definition is based on the following regulation:
Regulation (EU) 2025/40 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 19 December 2024 on packaging and packaging waste, amending Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 and Directive (EU) 2019/904, and repealing Directive 94/62/EC

nodes.label: trackingFacilities
nodes.description:
Information about tracking facilities.

These are mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2025/40:  
(69) As regards reusable packaging, in order to inform end users about reusability, the availability of re-use systems and the location of collection channels, such packaging should bear a QR code or other standardised, open, digital data carrier that provides such information. The QR code or data carrier should contain information which facilitates tracking and the calculation of trips and rotations, or an average estimation if such calculations are not feasible. That label should be voluntary for open loop systems which do not have a system operator. In addition, reusable sales packaging should be clearly identified at the point of sale.

nodes.label: exemption
nodes.description: Exemptions to the substance of concern. This attribute is mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products Article 7: (5) (c) provide duly justified exemptions for substances of concern or information elements from the information requirements referred to in the first subparagraph based on the technical feasibility or relevance of tracking substances of concern, the existence of analytical methods to detect and quantify them, the need to protect confidential business information or in other duly justified cases. Substances of concern within the meaning of Article 2(28), point (a), shall not be exempted if they are present in products, their relevant components or spare parts in a concentration above 0,1 % weight by weight.

nodes.label: hazardClassifications
nodes.description: The specification of the hazard classes. The specification of the hazard class. This attribute is mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products Annex I:(f) use of substances, and in particular the use of substances of concern, on their own, as constituents of substances or in mixtures, during the production process of products, or leading to their presence in products, including once these products become waste, and their impacts on human health and the environment; Further defined is this by Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16 December 2008 on classification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures, amending and repealing Directives 67/548/EEC and 1999/45/EC, and amending Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006. Defined in Article 2: 1. 'hazard class' means the nature of the physical, health or environmental hazard; 2. 'hazard category' means the division of criteria within each hazard class, specifying hazard severity; 5. 'hazard statement' means a phrase assigned to a hazard class and category that describes the nature of the hazards of a hazardous substance or mixture, including, where appropriate, the degree of hazard.

nodes.label: substanceOfConcernDocuments
nodes.description: Documentation accompanying the material.

nodes.label: substanceOfConcernLocations
nodes.description: Locations of the substance of concern within the product.

nodes.label: substanceOfConcernConcentrationRange
nodes.description: Based on the regulation: concentration range for the substance of concern.

nodes.label: declarableIngredientListName
nodes.description: Name of the Declarable Ingredient List.

nodes.label: declarableIngredientListDocumentId
nodes.description: Document ID refered to Declarable Ingredient list.

nodes.label: declarableIngredientDocuments
nodes.description: Documentation accompanying the Declarable Ingredient list with information related to declarable ingredients.

nodes.label: concentrationValue
nodes.description: Concentration of the material at the level of the product. This attribute is specially mentioned for substances of concern mentioned in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products, Article 7: (5)(c) the concentration, maximum concentration or concentration range of the substances of concern, at the level of the product, its relevant components, or spare parts; Other substances are mentioned for the purporse of recycling in the Regulation (EU) 2024/1781 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 establishing a framework for the setting of ecodesign requirements for sustainable products Annex I: (d) design for recycling, ease and quality of recycling as expressed through use of easily recyclable materials, safe, easy and non-destructive access to recyclable components and materials or components and materials containing hazardous substances and material composition and homogeneity, possibility for high-purity sorting, number of materials and components used, use of standard components, use of component and material coding standards for the identification of components and materials, number and complexity of processes and tools needed, ease of non-destructive disassembly and re-assembly, conditions for access to product data, conditions for access to or use of hardware and software needed.

nodes.label: concentrationUnit
nodes.description: The unit of concentration chosen from an enumeration: mass percent, volume percent, parts per thousand, parts per million, parts per billion and  parts per trillion.

nodes.label: minConcentration
nodes.description: The minimum concentration of the material at the level of the product.

nodes.label: maxConcentration
nodes.description: The maximum concentration of the material at the level of the product.

nodes.label: calculationKey
nodes.description: Key for calculation of trips/rotations.

nodes.label: calculationValue
nodes.description: Dynamic value for calculation trips/rotations.

nodes.label: hazardCategory
nodes.description:
The hazard category of the substance of concern. Defined in Article 2 of Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16 December 2008 on classification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures, amending and repealing Directives 67/548/EEC and 1999/45/EC, and amending Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006:
2. 'hazard category' means the division of criteria within each hazard class, specifying hazard severity.

nodes.label: hazardClass
nodes.description:
The hazard class of the substance of concern. Defined in Article 2 of Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16 December 2008 on classification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures, amending and repealing Directives 67/548/EEC and 1999/45/EC, and amending Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006:
1. 'hazard class' means the nature of the physical, health or environmental hazard.

nodes.label: hazardStatement
nodes.description:
The hazard statement of the substance of concern. Defined in Article 2 of Regulation (EC) No 1272/2008 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16 December 2008 on classification, labelling and packaging of substances and mixtures, amending and repealing Directives 67/548/EEC and 1999/45/EC, and amending Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006:
5. 'hazard statement' means a phrase assigned to a hazard class and category that describes the nature of the hazards of a hazardous substance or mixture, including, where appropriate, the degree of hazard.

nodes.label: MetadataCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the passport metadata of the digital product passport.

nodes.label: IdentificationCharacteristic
nodes.description: Identification information of the product.

nodes.label: OperationCharacteristic
nodes.description: Operational information of the product.

nodes.label: HandlingCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic to describe aspects which are connected with the handling of the product.

nodes.label: ProductCharacteristics
nodes.description: Defines a set of specific characteristics of a product.

nodes.label: CommercialCharacteristic
nodes.description: Commercial information of the product.

nodes.label: SustainabilityCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic which describes relevant information for the sustainability of the product.

nodes.label: CompositionCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for composition.

nodes.label: PredecessorCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic of the predecessor of the actual digital product passport.

nodes.label: IdentifierCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for Identifiers.

nodes.label: EconomicOperatorCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic of the economic operator.

nodes.label: PartTypeCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the part type.

nodes.label: ManufacturingCharacteristic
nodes.description: Manufacturing information.

nodes.label: ImportCharacteristic
nodes.description: Import characteristic for the product.

nodes.label: ExtendedProducerResponsibilitySchemeCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic of Extended Producer Responsibility Scheme.

nodes.label: PhysicalDimensionCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for physical dimensions with linear, mass and capacity attributes.

nodes.label: RecallInformationCharacteristic
nodes.description: Recall information characteristic for the product.

nodes.label: ProductFootprintCharacteristic
nodes.description: The Product Footprint Characteristic with sustainability information.

nodes.label: ReusabilityCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic of the reusability of the product.

nodes.label: RecycleabilityPerformanceCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for recycleability performance.

nodes.label: TypeCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic for the data type.

nodes.label: ComponentInformationCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic of component information.

nodes.label: MaterialInformationCharacteristic
nodes.description: Characteristic of the material information.

nodes.label: ManufacturerCharacteristic

nodes.description: The current status of the product passport declared through either: draft, approved, invalid or expired.

nodes.label: PhysicalStateEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration with the values solid, liquid, gas and plasma. Due to its specific character, aerosol was added.

nodes.label: ProductStatusEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration defined as original, repurposed, re-used, remanufactured or waste.

nodes.label: SourceCategoryEnumeration
nodes.description: The types of sources that can be linked in a digital product passport can vary depending on the nature of the product and the information that needs to be included. This is an enumeration of possible sources.

nodes.label: LifeEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration with the possible units day, month, cycle, year and runningOrOperatingHour.

nodes.label: LifeTypeEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration with the values guaranteed lifetime, technical lifetime and mean time between failures.

nodes.label: DataTypeEnumeration
nodes.description: Data type that describe the content of the attributes children and data. In case "object" is selected in the enumeration, the children field will be used in the AdditionalDataEntity instead of the "data" property. If it is an other type, the content will be specified in "data" as a string.

nodes.label: CategoryEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration of the 19 impact categories in accordance to EN15804+A2.

nodes.label: ListTypeIdEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration of different systems and organizations related to the identification and classification of chemical substances in the field of chemistry. The enumeration values are eg. EC or CAS.

nodes.label: ConcentrationEnumeration
nodes.description: Enumeration of possible units of concentration with percent, volume percent, parts per thousand, parts per million, parts per billion and parts per trillion.

nodes.label: DateTrait

nodes.description: Trait for a restrictive header.

nodes.description: Trait for positive values.

nodes.label: PercentageTrait
nodes.description: Trait for a positive percentage.

nodes.label: DateConstraint
nodes.description: Constraint fo a timestamp in the format (yyyy-mm-dd).

nodes.label: EoriConstraint
nodes.description: Constraint with a pattern which ensures that the EORI number starts with two alphanumeric characters, followed by two alphabetic characters, and ends with 2 to 15 alphanumeric characters.

nodes.label: HeaderConstraint
nodes.description: Constraint for a maximum of 100 characters.

nodes.label: LifeValueCharacteristic

nodes.label: FloatValue
nodes.description: Float number representing a positive value.

nodes.label: PositiveRangeConstraint
nodes.description: Constraint for only positive values.

nodes.description: Range constraint for a positive percentage value.
